title: US Bitcoin ETFs add $167M as altcoin funds extend outflows
description: US Bitcoin ETFs added $167 million in inflows on Monday, while Ether, XRP and Solana funds saw three-day outflows despite a crypto market rebound.

title: Blackstone-owned AirTrunk secures record $1.24B loan for Tokyo AI data center
description: A consortium of 12 banks, led by SMBC, MUFG, Crédit Agricole CIB and Société Générale, arranged the record financing for AirTrunk’s Tokyo data center expansion.

title: Tron joins Agentic AI Foundation as founder sees future in AI
description: Tron joined the Linux Foundation’s Agentic AI Foundation to help develop open infrastructure for AI agents alongside members including Circle and JPMorgan.

title: Bhutan moves $11.8M in BTC from its national stash: Arkham
description: Bhutan has accumulated around 13,000 Bitcoin since launching state-backed mining operations in 2019, primarily fueled by hydroelectric energy.

title: Vitalik Buterin envisions ‘one-click’ Ether staking for institutions
description: “We want the authority over staking nodes to be highly distributed, and the first step to doing this is to make it easy,” said Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin.

title: Banks Need CLARITY Act More Than Crypto – Former CFTC Chair Explains Why
description: US banks may need regulatory clarity more than the crypto industry, a former Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) chief said, arguing they risk falling behind the rest of the world. title: Bithumb Faces 6-Month Suspension In South Korea Over AML, KYC Violations
description: The South Korea-based cryptocurrency exchange, Bithumb, is facing significant legal and operational challenges following a major system error in February. title: Kraken Partners With Nasdaq In New Tokenized Stocks Move
description: Kraken parent Payward has partnered with Nasdaq to build what the companies describe as an “equities transformation gateway,” a new infrastructure layer designed to connect regulated tokenized equity markets with permissionless blockchain networks. For crypto markets, the significance is clear: one of the largest traditional market operators is now working directly with a crypto-native tokenization […]

title: Wall Street Banks Weigh Lawsuit Over Crypto Banking Charters
description: The potential lawsuit adds to existing tension between traditional lenders and crypto firms over access to the U.S. financial system.

title: Trump's DOJ Seeks October Retrial for Tornado Cash Developer Roman Storm
description: The DOJ is attempting to retry Tornado Cash developer Roman Storm even as the U.S. Treasury acknowledges mixers may have legitimate uses.

title: Elon Musk’s Grok Faces UK Backlash After AI Posts Mock Football Tragedies
description: Liverpool and Manchester United have complained after Grok mocked the Hillsborough and Munich tragedies on Monday.

title: Bitcoin Shows ‘Tentative Signs of Improvement’ as Iran Conflict Fears Wane
description: Oil’s sharp swing and renewed Bitcoin ETF inflows highlight a fragile rebound as on-chain data suggest crypto market stress may be easing.

title: Americans Use AI Every Day—But Most Still Don't Like It, New Poll Shows
description: Americans are increasingly using AI tools even as a majority say the technology’s risks outweigh its benefits.

title: Uzbekistan’s Uzum valuation leaps over 50% in seven months to $2.3B
description: Uzum now reaches 20 million users and processes $11B in payments as it expands its e-commerce and fintech ecosystem across Uzbekistan.

title: Apple now makes one in four iPhones in India: report
description: Apple reportedly produced 55 million units in India last year, a shift accelerated as Apple faced growing uncertainty in China.

title: Yann LeCun’s AMI Labs raises $1.03 billion to build world models
description: AMI Labs, the new venture cofounded by Turing Prize winner Yann LeCun after he left Meta, has raised $1.03 billion at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation.

title: An iPhone-hacking toolkit used by Russian spies likely came from U.S military contractor
description: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.

title: Electric air taxi maker Archer hits back at Joby in countersuit alleging concealed Chinese ties
description: Archer's countersuit comes four months after Joby sued its rival for alleged 'corporate espionage.'

title: Yann LeCun Raises $1 Billion to Build AI That Understands the Physical World
description: Meta’s former chief AI scientist has long argued that human-level AI will come from mastering the physical world, not language. His new startup, AMI, aims to prove it.

title: Nvidia Is Planning to Launch an Open-Source AI Agent Platform
description: Ahead of its annual developer conference, Nvidia is readying a new approach to software that embraces AI agents similar to OpenClaw.

title: ‘Flying Cars’ Will Take Off in American Skies This Summer
description: The federal government announced a new pilot program designed to get new kinds of ultralight vehicles and “eVTOLs” up and running around the country—even if they're not fully FAA-certified.

title: Anthropic Claims Pentagon Feud Could Cost It Billions
description: Executives at the AI startup say companies paused deal talks after the Trump administration labeled it a supply-chain risk, warning that the fallout could cause a major revenue hit.

title: OpenAI and Google Workers File Amicus Brief in Support of Anthropic Against the US Government
description: Google DeepMind chief scientist Jeff Dean is among the AI researchers and engineers rushing to Anthropic's defense.

title: Show HN: I Was Here – Draw on street view, others can find your drawings
description:
Hey HN, I made a site where you can draw on street-level panoramas. Your drawings persist and other people can see them in real time.

Strokes get projected onto the 3D panorama so they wrap around buildings and follow the geometry, not just a flat overlay. Uses WebGL2 for rendering, Mapillary for the street imagery.

The idea is for it to become a global canvas, anyone can leave a mark anywhere and others stumble onto it.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47319255
